siblings: ult-combo table (synergistic ults — still a stub)
meta.purpose: Read YOUR team's live picks + teammate playstyle, find functional gaps in the comp, and recommend a pick/swap from the user's comfort pool that closes the biggest gap.
meta.compliance: OUTPUT IS ALWAYS A PICK OR SWAP, NEVER A BAN. Uses only your-side roster data (character_name) + optional public profile history for playstyle. No enemy confidential data.
meta.shelf_life: Function tags are fairly durable; re-check after major reworks. Strength weighting comes from the patch overlay, not here.
meta.engine_note: Same weighted set-cover as the counter engine. There it covers enemy threats; here it covers comp functions. The two can run together: weight a gap higher when the enemy comp punishes it (cross-reference counter_kb).

frontline_space.label: Frontline / space control
frontline_space.why: Hold or take ground; without it the team gets run over.

sustain_healing.label: Sustain / healing throughput
sustain_healing.why: Keep the team alive through fights; <2 strategists usually folds.

anti_dive_peel.label: Anti-dive / backline peel
anti_dive_peel.why: Protect squishies from divers; the classic 'answer hero' role.

anti_flyer_grounding.label: Anti-flyer / grounding
anti_flyer_grounding.why: Answer to permaflyers (Human Torch, Storm, Angela, Iron Man).

burst_pick.label: Burst / pick threat
burst_pick.why: Ability to delete a priority target and break a stalemate.

range_poke.label: Poke / range
range_poke.why: Win neutral and chip from distance before committing.

dive_flank.label: Dive / flank pressure
dive_flank.why: Threaten the enemy backline; forces their peel.

zone_wall.label: Zone / wall presence or break
zone_wall.why: Deny space (Groot/Peni/Squirrel) or break walls; situational.

anti_heal.label: Anti-heal
anti_heal.why: Counter sustain-brawl (Dino, Blade); situational but swingy.

ult_denial_defensive.label: Defensive / ult-denial ult
ult_denial_defensive.why: A defensive ult to survive or trade enemy ults.

hero_functions.comment: Full roster (Phase 0, A4 parity with counter_kb.heroes). Each hero lists the functions it MEANINGFULLY provides (not every theoretical use). EXCEPTION: the Deadpool role-split keys (Deadpool_Tank/DPS/Strat) are intentionally absent — GEP emits one 'Deadpool' name (A3); they are allow-listed in the validator until role disambiguation is resolved in the GEP spike.

playstyle_modifiers.comment: Detect your comp's archetype from the function distribution of teammate picks, then re-weight gap priorities. Playstyle can also be inferred per-teammate from their public most-played heroes.

dive.note: Dive needs follow-up burst and a frontline that enables it; static poke supports clash with it.
dive.counter_read: They'll collapse your backline — value peel and a CC-immune frontline; group so divers can't pick you off alone.

triple-support.note: A heal-stack out-sustains a straight DPS race. Precedence: checked BEFORE brawl, since a triple-heal comp often also satisfies the brawl trigger.
triple-support.counter_read: They out-sustain you — a damage race loses. Prioritize anti-heal, deny their support ults, and dive their backline to remove a healer.

brawl.note: Brawl mirrors are won with anti-heal + zone control; vulnerable if backline unprotected.
brawl.counter_read: Sustained close fights — anti-heal and zone control swing the mirror; don't feed their frontline.

poke.note: Poke comps are dive-bait; they need peel and grounding or they get collapsed.
poke.counter_read: They win from range — close distance and break sightlines; bring dive or a shield to force them off their angles.

critical_gap.example: Team has no anti-dive and two immobile supports -> they will get dove and die.

redundancy.rule: providers(function) > ideal_providers + 1 while some other ideal function == 0
redundancy.action: suggest rebalancing one redundant pick toward the empty function.

examples: immobile poke support (e.g. Mantis) inside a >=3 dive comp
two slow brawl tanks in a comp built around flank pressure
clash.action: flag the clash; suggest the pick that resolves it from the user's pool.

steps: 1. Tag each teammate's live character_name with its functions; aggregate coverage counts.
2. Detect archetype via playstyle_modifiers; apply raise/lower weights to gap priorities.
3. (optional) Cross-reference counter_kb: raise the weight of any gap the ENEMY comp punishes.
4. Compute prioritized gap list (critical first, then weighted ideal shortfalls).
5. Intersect needed functions with the user's comfort pool (heroes they actually play).
6. Pick the user-pool hero covering the most top-priority gap weight with one change (set cover; prefer a single pick that fills 2+ gaps).
7. Output a PICK (draft phase) or SWAP (mid-match) suggestion with the gap it closes. NEVER a ban.

output_example.suggestion: From your pool: pick Luna — adds the 2nd healer AND a defensive ult the dive comp wants. If you'd rather hold DPS, Punisher covers the missing grounding.
